In 2015, Cho's talent shone brightly as he won the prestigious International Chopin Piano Competition, cementing his place among the world's top pianists.
activity_question: What major competition did Seong-Jin Cho win in 2015?
follow_up_question: Can you name another famous classical composer besides Chopin?

title: Splashing into History: London's First Indoor Pool
story: Swimmers, get ready to dive in! In 1742, London made a big splash by opening its very first indoor swimming pool. Located in Goodman's Fields, this groundbreaking pool gave people a chance to enjoy water fun no matter the weather. On May 28, Londoners celebrated this exciting new way to stay active and cool off, even when it was raining outside.
activity_question: Where was London's first indoor swimming pool located?
follow_up_question: Why do you think indoor pools became popular?

title: Flames Engulf a Canadian Town
story: Flames roared through the streets of Quebec, Canada in 1845. On May 28, a massive fire swept across the city, leaving destruction in its wake. The blaze spread quickly, fueled by wooden buildings common at the time. When the smoke cleared, about 1,500 houses lay in ruins. Despite the devastation, the people of Quebec banded together to rebuild their community.
activity_question: What material made the fire spread so quickly?
follow_up_question: How might cities prevent fires from spreading so easily today?

title: Azerbaijan's Bold Step to Freedom
story: Freedom rang out across Azerbaijan in 1918! On May 28, this Caucasus nation took a brave leap, declaring itself the Democratic Republic of Azerbaijan. It was a historic moment as the country embraced independence, stepping away from Russian control. The people celebrated their new path of self-governance and democracy.
activity_question: What did Azerbaijan declare itself as in 1918?
follow_up_question: Can you find Azerbaijan on a map?

title: Cricket Star's Lightning-Fast Milestone
story: Bat meets ball with a resounding crack! In 1927, cricket fans cheered as Wally Hammond reached an incredible milestone. The talented batsman scored his 1,000th run of the season on May 28, after just 22 days of play. Hammond's swift achievement showcased his exceptional skill and thrilled spectators across England.
activity_question: How many days did it take Hammond to score 1,000 runs?
follow_up_question: What sport do you think requires the most endurance?

title: Lights, Camera, Color!
story: Moviegoers gasped in amazement as vibrant colors danced across the screen. The year was 1929, and on May 28, New York City witnessed a cinematic revolution. 'On With the Show' made history as the first all-color talking picture ever exhibited. This groundbreaking film brought a whole new dimension to storytelling, combining the magic of sound with a dazzling rainbow of hues.
activity_question: What was special about the movie 'On With the Show'?
activity_choices: It was the first all-color talking picture
It was the longest movie ever made
activity_answer: It was the first all-color talking picture
follow_up_question: How do you think movies changed after this breakthrough?

title: Nature's Fury Strikes Bangladesh
story: Winds howled and waves crashed as a powerful cyclone slammed into Chittagong, Bangladesh in 1963. The storm's fierce strength toppled homes like dominoes, leaving about a million houses in ruins on May 28. Despite the destruction, the resilient people of Bangladesh began rebuilding, determined to overcome nature's challenge.
activity_question: What city in Bangladesh was hit by the cyclone?
follow_up_question: How can communities prepare for natural disasters like cyclones?
